title: Madhavi is a trained and publicly recognised artist
claim_or_observation: Madhavi is presented as an accomplished performer whose authority rests on extensive artistic training and public recognition

title: Madhavi turns toward renunciation after Kovalan's death
claim_or_observation: Madhavi reorganises her life after Kovalan's death and withdraws from the expected public performance role
notes: Verify exact acts terminology and chronology

title: Madhavi seeks a different future for Manimekalai
claim_or_observation: Madhavi directs her daughter away from the inherited performance and patronage future
notes: Distinguish direct command from inferred intention

title: Madhavi's withdrawal becomes public knowledge
claim_or_observation: The absence of Madhavi and Manimekalai from expected public participation generates discussion and pressure

title: Madhavi's history is inseparable from Kovalan and Manimekalai
claim_or_observation: Madhavi's relationship with Kovalan and motherhood of Manimekalai shape her public memory and present decisions

title: Madurai's failed justice restructures Madhavi's life
claim_or_observation: Kovalan's execution and the catastrophe surrounding Madurai form the historical and emotional rupture preceding Madhavi's renunciation
notes: Do not convert cross-epic causation into modern clinical diagnosis

title: Chitrapathi contests the household's withdrawal
claim_or_observation: Chitrapathi acts to preserve the performance household and restore Manimekalai to elite public visibility
notes: Verify kinship title motives and actions

title: Grief reorganises household routine
claim_or_observation: Madhavi's grief is performed through changed rooms schedules work patron access dress and speech rather than continuous lament

title: Maternal protection can reproduce control
claim_or_observation: Madhavi's effort to protect Manimekalai may become another imposed future when she treats her own solution as the daughter's obligation

title: Renunciation is not repentance for female impurity
claim_or_observation: Madhavi's renunciation must not imply that performance sexuality motherhood or her body made her morally impure
notes: Binding religious gender and publicity rule

title: Madhavi's occupational category has no approved modern equivalent
claim_or_observation: Terms translated as courtesan cannot be equated automatically with devadasi prostitution concubinage slavery or celebrity
notes: Binding terminology casting and production rule

title: Madhavi's body retains artistic training
claim_or_observation: Balance rhythm breath spatial control and gaze plausibly remain after public performance ends
notes: Do not claim unsupported modern dance vocabulary

title: Madhavi's voice carries former public projection
claim_or_observation: Her quieter renunciant speech still carries breath control timing and authority developed through performance

title: Renunciation affects household workers and dependants
claim_or_observation: Ending public performance changes income patron relationships work routines and obligations beyond Madhavi alone

title: Kannagi and Madhavi are not moral opposites
claim_or_observation: The adaptation must not construct a pure wife versus fallen performer hierarchy between Kannagi and Madhavi

title: No destruction of artistic objects without textual support
claim_or_observation: Instruments costumes jewellery and teaching objects must not be theatrically destroyed as shorthand for renunciation unless the text supports it
notes: Binding props costume and screenplay rule

title: Madhavi's mature arc releases control without disappearing
claim_or_observation: Her development culminates in remaining available as mother and witness while allowing Manimekalai's vocation to be self-chosen
